items.headline: TheFork Joins AmEx's Growing Global Dining Network in $700M Deal
items.summary: American Express will acquire TheFork from Tripadvisor for about $700M, expanding its dining network to roughly 75,000 bookable venues across Europe.
items.why_it_matters: News impact is unclear; monitor price and volume reaction.

items.headline: Tripadvisor sale of TheFork seen as accretive to valuation not outlook, says Jefferies
items.summary: Tripadvisor Inc (NASDAQ:TRIP) has agreed to sell its European restaurant reservations platform TheFork to American Express for $700 million in cash, a move that Jefferies says simplifies the company’s structure but does not fully offset longer-term pressure in its core business. items.headline: JPMorgan, Google Records Sought in Probe Into Epstein’s New Mexico Ranch
items.summary: New Mexico’s Justice Department ordered banks and other companies to preserve records tied to Jeffrey Epstein and his associates.

items.headline: TRIP Stock Gains Premarket As Analysts Raise Tripadvisor Targets – See Value Creation From TheFork Deal
items.summary: Analysts highlighted the financial flexibility the company could gain from the divestiture of TheFork.
items.why_it_matters: News appears more supportive of sentiment or growth expectations.

items.headline: American Express Expands Dining Ecosystem With TheFork Acquisition From Tripadvisor
items.summary: American Express (NYSE:AXP) agreed to acquire TheFork, Tripadvisor's European restaurant reservation platform, for US$700 million in cash. TheFork operates in 11 European countries and connects users with more than 50,000 restaurants. The deal is expected to close by late 2026, subject to regulatory approvals. American Express (NYSE:AXP) is increasing its focus on dining and experiences, adding TheFork's more than 50,000 European restaurants to its existing network. Once completed, the...
